---
title: "Causal Inference"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Causal Inference}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::knitr}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r}
library(ppiAIPW)

set.seed(2)
n <- 120
x <- rnorm(n)
A <- sample(c("control", "treated"), n, replace = TRUE)
mu0 <- 0.5 + 0.6 * x
mu1 <- mu0 + 0.8
Y <- ifelse(A == "treated", mu1, mu0) + rnorm(n, sd = 0.4)
Yhat_potential <- cbind(control = mu0, treated = mu1)

result <- causal_inference(
  Y,
  A,
  Yhat_potential,
  method = "linear",
  control_arm = "control"
)

print(result)
summary(result)
```

The causal wrapper treats each arm-specific mean as a semi-supervised mean problem
under the hood, then forms control-vs-treatment ATEs from the arm estimates and
their Wald covariance.
